title: The Lock and the Other Way
description: Whispering Cave, ch.3. A locked chest. The Rogue finds the way through without making it look like effort.

steps.text: Three chambers in, there was a chest. The lock was the kind that announced itself: brass, oversized, with a keyhole shaped like a question.

rogue.text: The lead was already crouched beside it. They had been crouched beside it for a while. They had a thin piece of wire and were not explaining what they were doing.
The lock clicked. The lid lifted. The lead did not look up. They never did.

default.text: The Rogue did not say anything. The Rogue was already kneeling beside the lock, a thin piece of wire in one hand, the other resting flat on the chest's lid the way a doctor rests a hand on a patient.
Three seconds. Four. The lock clicked — not loud, not satisfying, just *over.* The Rogue stood up and stepped back.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Don't ask how. You won't like the answer.

default.text: The Rogue had a way of telling you a thing was done without explaining how the thing got done. The Rogue was, the party would learn, the kind of person who found the second way through every door.
